reasoning: The Lean statement correctly encodes all pieces of the informal claim. `E P : Set (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k))` faithfully represents subsets of $\mathbb{R}^k$. The hypothesis `hE : ¬ Set.Countable E` matches "$E$ is uncountable." The definition `hP : P = {x | ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)}` captures the standard Rudin definition of condensation point: every neighborhood of $x$ contains uncountably many points of $E$ (using the neighborhood filter is equivalent to "every open ball / every neighborhood" since $\mathbb{R}^k$ is a metric space). The conclusion `Set.Countable (E \ P)` is exactly "at most countably many points of $E$ are not in $P$." No hypothesis is silently added or dropped, and the quantifier structure matches.
